2605.00650.title: AdaMeZO: Adam-style Zeroth-Order Optimizer for LLM Fine-tuning Without Maintaining the Moments
2605.00650.paper_summary: Fine-tuning LLMs is necessary for various dedicated downstream tasks, but classic backpropagation-based fine-tuning methods require substantial GPU memory. To this end, a recent work, MeZO, which relies solely on forward passes to fine-tune LLMs, significantly reduces GPU requirements at the cost of slower convergence due to its indifference to loss landscapes. Standard solutions, such as Adam, explore loss landscapes by estimating the first- and second-order moments and storing them in memory to guide the model's movement through dimensions with lower curvature and vice versa. However, directly applying Adam negates MeZO's advantage as it will triple the memory requirement. In light of this, we propose AdaMeZO, a zeroth-order optimizer that leverages Adam-style first- and second-moment estimates without maintaining them in memory. We present a theoretical analysis of AdaMeZO, corroborated by extensive experiments demonstrating AdaMeZO's performance, showing that AdaMeZO can outperform MeZO while requiring up to $70\%$ fewer forward passes. Trajectory visualizations affirm AdaMeZO's ability to adapt to diverse loss landscapes.
2605.00650.paper_summary_zh: 微調 LLM 是各種專用下游任務所必需的，但經典的基於反向傳播的微調方法需要大量的 GPU 記憶體。為此，最近的一項工作 MeZO 僅依賴前向傳播來微調 LLM，顯著降低了 GPU 的需求，但由於對損失景觀的漠不關心，導致收斂速度變慢。標準解決方案，如 Adam，通過估計一階和二階矩來探索損失景觀，並將它們存儲在記憶體中，以指導模型在低曲率的維度中移動，反之亦然。然而，直接應用 Adam 會抵消 MeZO 的優勢，因為這將使記憶體需求增加三倍。鑒於此，我們提出了 AdaMeZO，一種零階優化器，利用 Adam 風格的一階和二階矩估計，而不在記憶體中維護它們。我們對 AdaMeZO 進行了理論分析，並通過大量實驗證實了 AdaMeZO 的性能，顯示 AdaMeZO 可以在需要最多 $70\%$ 更少的前向傳播次數的情況下超越 MeZO。軌跡可視化證實了 AdaMeZO 適應多樣化損失景觀的能力。
2605.00650.author: Zhijie Cai et.al.
2605.00650.authors: Zhijie Cai, Haolong Chen, Guangxu Zhu
